channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Judge asks potential jurors if they can be fair toward 3 officers charged in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Potential jurors in the federal trial of three former Memphis police officers charged with violating Tyre Nichols ’ civil rights were asked Monday whether heavy media coverage and publicly released video of him being beaten to death could influence their ability to be fair. U.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Diddy lists LA mansion raided by Homeland Security for $61M
item.description: DHS agents conducted the raids on March 25 at Combs’ multimillion-dollar mansion in Los Angeles as well as his Miami waterfront home.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Francis will be only the second pope to visit Singapore, one of the wealthiest nations
item.description: SINGAPORE (AP) — When Pope Francis arrives Wednesday in Asia’s financial powerhouse Singapore for the last leg of a four-nation tour, he is expected to bring his message of unity and hope to one of the world’s richest nations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: AP-NORC/USAFacts poll: Republicans are more likely to trust Trump than official election results
item.description: Republicans are more likely to trust Donald Trump and his campaign over government certified election results, according to a new survey from the Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research and USAFacts.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Congress honors 13 troops killed during Kabul withdrawal as politics swirl around who is to blame
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson is hosting a ceremony to posthumously present Congress’ highest honor — the Congressional Gold Medal — to 13 U.S. service members who were killed during the disastrous withdrawal from Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves And Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What we know about rapper Rich Homie Quan's death
item.description: An autopsy was performed Friday on Quan, but it could take months before medical examiner releases cause of death.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Widow of Johnny Gaudreau reveals she's pregnant with their 3rd child at his memorial
item.description: During the memorial service Monday for her husband, Johnny, Meredith Gaudreau announced that she is pregnant.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A judge agrees to move the trial of a man charged with killing 4 University of Idaho students
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — The judge overseeing the trial of the man charged in the fatal stabbings of four University of Idaho students has agreed to move it out of the small city where the shocking crimes occurred, citing concerns about finding impartial jurors and whether the courthouse could accommodat
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE and GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How past debates set up Harris, Trump for Tuesday's showdown
item.description: Trump was disciplined in his most recent debate against Biden, while Harris has been respectful yet assertive in previous VP debates.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-officer's lies led to couple's death in Houston drug raid, prosecutor tells jurors
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A former Houston police officer is responsible for the 2019 deaths of a couple during a raid of their home because his lies on a search warrant wrongly portrayed them as dangerous drug dealers, a prosecutor told jurors on Monday.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tropical Storm Francine expected to be a hurricane by Wednesday
item.description: Forecasters expect Tropical Storm Francine to grow to a hurricane and hit the Louisiana coast on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Vance pushes false accusations of Haitians eating pets
item.description: GOP vice presidential nominee Sen. JD Vance (Ohio) on Monday amplified a false claim that Haitian immigrants are abducting and eating pets in Springfield, Ohio, despite the city’s police department denial of any such incidents.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-employees of Titanic submersible's owner to testify before Coast Guard panel
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Former employees of the company that owned an experimental submersible that imploded on its way to the wreck of the Titanic are scheduled to testify before a Coast Guard investigatory board at an upcoming hearing.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Arrest of illegal immigrant previously convicted of rape in Maryland marks record for ICE
item.description: An illegal immigrant from Honduras was arrested again after entering the United States, authorities said last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Border residents still on lookout for tiger that escaped from Mexican zoo
item.description: Officials in South Texas and northern Mexico remain on the lookout for a Bengal tiger that escaped from a zoo in the Mexican border town of Reynosa last week.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Student arrested for making online threat to Connecticut school
item.description: Meriden police have arrested an Edison Middle School student for allegedly making a threat against fellow students, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Schaefer, NewsNation

item.title: Harvey Weinstein taken to NYC hospital for emergency heart surgery: representatives
item.description: Jailed ex-movie mogul Harvey Weinstein underwent emergency heart surgery at a New York City hospital on Monday, his representatives said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: LL Flooring does about-face after securing 11th-hour buyer; will keep some stores
item.description: After securing a last-minute buyer, LL Flooring is reversing course on shutting down all of its stores.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Rescuers save man trapped in 36-inch drainpipe in Southern California
item.description: A harrowing rescue unfolded amid sweltering temperatures as first responders in Ventura County, California, worked to free a man from a 36-inch drainpipe Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Big Lots files for bankruptcy, plans to close hundreds of locations
item.description: Popular discount retailer Big Lots has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. As part of the bankruptcy and restructuring, 295 stores have already begun to shut down in what the company says is "optimizing our store footprint.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Man accused of shooting 5 on Kentucky interstate vowed to 'kill a lot of people,' warrant says
item.description: An arrest warrant says the man accused of opening fire on a Kentucky highway vowed to “kill a lot of people” before he shot and wounded five people.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schreiner, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Government shutdown looms as Congress faces funding fight
item.description: Congress is staring down a time crunch to avert a shutdown, with both parties digging in their heels and some Republicans expressing skepticism about their own party’s plans less than a month before government funding is set to run out. Lawmakers return to Washington on Monday facing a Sept.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, WGN 9

item.title: Tennessee man cited for having too many flags in yard
item.description: A Franklin resident is asking city leaders to reconsider an ordinance after he received a citation for having too many flags in his front yard.
item.dc:creator: Nikki McGee, ABC 4

item.title: READ IT: Woman who 'burnt bridges' gets short obituary
item.description: A short and not-so-sweet obituary posted in a Maine newspaper last week is making national headlines and the daughter who penned the final tribute tells PEOPLE it's "no joke.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Tyreek Hill detained before Dolphins game; 1 officer subsequently placed on leave
item.description: Hill was seen on video posted to the social media site X laying face down on the ground as officers placed his hands behind his back and appeared to put handcuffs on him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Kentucky interstate shooting: Suspect at large as investigators find gun, SUV
item.description: While a car and firearm have been located, investigators are continuing to search for the suspect in a Saturday night shooting that injured five people in rural Kentucky.
item.dc:creator: Corey Elam, Fox 5

item.title: Is it too early to get a flu shot?
item.description: Health experts, who you've often hear urging vaccination as soon as possible, are instead suggesting it may not be the ideal time for most people to get their annual influenza vaccine.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Evacuations ordered as wildfire burns in foothills of national forest east of LA
item.description: SAN BERNARDINO, Calif. (AP) — Evacuations were ordered Saturday as a wildfire scorched the foothills of a national forest east of Los Angeles, amid a days-long heat wave that pushed temperatures into the triple digits across the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wynn Resorts paying $130M for letting illegal money reach gamblers at its Las Vegas Strip casino
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Casino company Wynn Resorts Ltd. has agreed to pay $130 million to federal authorities and admit that it let unlicensed money transfer businesses around the world funnel funds to gamblers at its flagship Las Vegas Strip property.
item.dc:creator: KEN RITTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man mauled to death by dogs in California, 1 arrested
item.description: When officers arrived at the scene, they found the victim suffering from several dog bites, police said.
item.dc:creator: Rob Nesbitt, Fox 5

item.title: When can customers expect the next Costco price hike?
item.description: When can customers expect the next price hike? The former CFO had previously laid out a possible timeline.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: US higher education advocates welcome federal support for Hispanic-serving institutions
item.description: Higher education advocates in Latino communities say they are optimistic about a new federal effort to support hundreds of local colleges and universities.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to pick the best preschool or child care center for your child
item.description: Preschool options are growing with the expansion of early learning programs, giving families in some parts of the U.S. a dizzying array of choices. Some states, like Colorado and New Mexico, are funneling state money into existing private preschools or child care centers.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harris spokesperson jabs Fox News host for acting 'like she's in the witness protection program'
item.description: “She’s been travelin’ across the battleground states, talkin’ to thousands of people in packed arenas,” Sams, who previously served as spokesperson for oversight in the White House Counsel’s Office, added.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: VIDEO: Assistant Principal, teacher arrested after student put in chokehold at Florida middle school, deputies say
item.description: An Assistant Principal and teacher at a Tampa middle school were arrested in connection to a child abuse investigation after a video showed the teacher put a child in a chokehold, according to the Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: Walz says Gaza demonstrators are protesting for 'all the right reasons' while condemning Hamas
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice presidential candidate Tim Walz said Thursday that those protesting American support for Israel's war against Hamas in Gaza are doing so for “all the right reasons," as the Democratic ticket looks to balance its support for Israel with the humanitarian plight of civilians in t
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trader Joe's viral mini totes to return to stores in September
item.description: Trader Joe’s mini totes, which went viral on social media earlier this year, will be back in stock later this month.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
